blocks.content: It was a gloomy day.
Shirone packed his clothes, dry food, and emergency supplies into a military backpack, preparing for any situation.
He grabbed the fully packed backpack with his right hand and placed it into the slot of his Cubrick.
Holding Armand, he inspected the scabbard and stored it in the second slot of the Cubrick.
The total weight was 38.7 kilograms. It was packed optimally, leaving some spare space.
Shirone checked the room to see if he had forgotten anything and then checked the time.
Seeing that it was close to midnight, he took out Maya's letter from the desk drawer and read it again.
Maya had fallen for Shirone on the day of the Paranormal Psychic Science Research Society's presentation.
When Shirone stood in front of Maya at the graduation welcome party, it felt like fate.
The question about who he wanted to kiss was half a joke, half a wishful thought, hoping he might choose her.
Her feelings were fully expressed in the four-page letter. It was a sincere confession, devoid of embellishments.
If you accept my feelings, come to the park at midnight in two days.
Even if you don't come, I will never resent or hate you.
I hope you show your true feelings this time. I love you.
Shirone folded the letter along the crease Maya had made and put it back in the envelope. He carefully placed it in his inner pocket, pressing it gently with his palm.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Maya...
Shirone left the dormitory early, not wanting to keep her waiting on such a day.
He arrived at the park ten minutes early.
Under the only lit streetlamp, he waited for Maya to arrive.
Dong. Dong. Dong.
The bell tolled midnight.
Dark clouds thickened, and a storm rolled in. Thunder rumbled in the distance, and cold rain began to fall.
Swoosh!
The sound of countless percussion instruments disoriented Shirone's hearing.
Water streamed down from his wet hair, and his clothes grew heavier as they soaked up the rain.
Shirone watched the path Maya would take.
Dong.
The bell tolled 1 AM.
Maya still hadn't arrived.
He heard the sound of someone walking through the puddles behind him.
Only when the rain stopped falling on his head did Shirone turn around.
Amy stood there, holding an umbrella.
They just stared at each other.
Amy hoped Shirone would speak first, but he had nothing to say to her.
Amy's suppressed emotions finally exploded.
Resentment, anger, confusion, betrayal, and perhaps something more desperate.
Clenching her teeth, she spoke in a trembling voice.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Why are you here?
Shirone didn't answer.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Tell me. Why are you here? Are you really with Maya...

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This has nothing to do with you.
Amy's heart sank.
She knew Maya was going to confess.
She had agreed, confident in her understanding of Shirone's feelings.
She regretted it, fearing Maya's courage.
Unable to bear it, she had run to the park.
Shirone was waiting for Maya. And now, he was looking at her with cold eyes.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: No, it can't be.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Something's wrong, right? You've been acting strange lately. You're strange now. Tell me! What's going on? If you don't tell me, I won't see you anymore.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Fine. I don't care.
Amy couldn't believe it.
Why was he being so cold?
This wasn't the Shirone she knew. She was scared.
For the first time, she felt completely exposed in front of Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Why? Why are you doing this? Why won't you tell me anything? Is it because I'm not like Maya? You fool, I... I also...
Shirone hugged Amy tightly. He had no other way to hide his contorted face.
Hot tears mixed with the cold rain as they streamed down his cheeks.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Amy, thank you for everything.
Overwhelmed with sorrow, Amy burst into tears.
She tried to push him away, but her hands slipped.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: No. Don't hug me. Tell me! Why are you doing this? You jerk!
She had no strength left.
Time pushed her forward, and a cliff awaited her. For the first time, she wished she could turn back time.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Goodbye, Amy...
Thud.
The umbrella fell from Amy's hand. The sound of the rain grew louder.
Even as Shirone walked away, she stood there, unable to move, her face blank.
Tears filled her eyes, blinding her.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Ugh. Ugh.
As the sobs stuck in her throat burst out, the sky roared, crying for her.
Thud! Thud!
Graduation class head teacher Collie woke up to the sound of knocking on the annex door.
Hurriedly putting on a robe and rubbing his sleepy eyes, he opened the door to find Maya, drenched in rain.

blocks.speaker: Collie
blocks.content: Maya? What are you doing here at this hour?

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Teacher...
Unable to control her emotions, Maya threw herself into Collie's arms and burst into tears.
Waaah!
Collie was taken aback but checked Maya's condition. Calming her shivering body seemed the first priority.
Collie lit a fire in the fireplace, sat Maya down, and handed her a towel.
She made cocoa and placed it in front of her, but Maya just stared blankly at the flames.
She went.
Collie realized today was the day Maya had mentioned.
During the graduation schedule, she didn't show it, but she had a lot of affection for Maya, whom she had personally enrolled and taught at the magic school.
A delicate child. Always considering others' feelings, neglecting her own.
Collie first saw Maya at the opening performance of singer Canary's outdoor concert.
Commoners had the chance to watch from outside the barricades, and the nobles seemed to like this arrangement.
So the concert planner decided to have a commoner perform the opening act to highlight Canary's excellence.
That's how 14-year-old Maya was recruited.
But the planner's scheme backfired. The 14-year-old's popular song was so beautiful it gave everyone goosebumps.
The nobles were naturally displeased and began booing and criticizing as soon as the song ended.
Some even demanded the tribe chief bring Maya to their bed for a large sum of money.
The chief fought back and was beaten and driven away, and Maya gave up her dream of becoming a singer that day.
Collie suggested Maya become a mage.
Though delicate, she had undeniable talent and succeeded in entering the spirit zone within two years.
She didn't expect Maya to become a great mage.
If she could become a mage, uplift her tribe, and someday sing the songs she truly wanted, that would be enough.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Shirone didn't come out?
Maya smiled faintly. It was the only happiness she could find in her pain.

blocks.speaker: Collie
blocks.content: No. He came out. Foolishly, I told him not to. He could have cut ties with me, but he came out. He waited for me for over an hour.
Maya covered her face with her hands.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: And it hurts. Because I like him so much. Because I want to be his woman.

blocks.speaker: Collie
blocks.content: Why didn't you talk to him? Maybe he came out because he really likes you.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I knew from the start I couldn't win Shirone's heart. I confessed anyway. I liked him so much, I couldn't bear not to say it. So I walked into hell, knowing it.
Maya looked at Collie with a terrified expression.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: But what do I do? It hurts so much. It hurts so much I feel like I'm going to die. Can I forget Shirone? Can I go back to who I was before I met him?

blocks.speaker: Collie
blocks.content: Maya, you can't erase past memories. It's something you'll carry with you for life.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I don't think I can. How can I live with this pain...

blocks.speaker: Collie
blocks.content: There's no need to be afraid. The scars won't disappear, but as you live, you'll get so many new ones that you won't know which ones Shirone left. And then you'll be okay. You'll just live.
Maya buried her face in Collie's chest.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: That's not living. How can I live without any hope?

blocks.speaker: Collie
blocks.content: Maya, we don't live because of hope. We create hope to live. You may never forget, but it'll be okay. You'll create new hope.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I don't want that. I want Shirone to come to me. I want Shirone to love me.
When cut by a knife, it doesn't hurt immediately. The real pain comes when you realize you've been cut.
So what Maya felt now was not pain but fear. Fear of the pain to come.

The more delicate the heart, the harder it is. But Maya, an artist is someone who tells human stories. You can't see humans from high above. So remember today's pain. Sing from the lowest place. Then someday, everyone will soothe your wounds.
Collie patted Maya's back.
Her shoulders trembled pitifully, like a baby bird with broken wings in a storm.
3 AM.
Shirone had never intended to sleep, so he returned to his dorm, washed, and changed into new clothes.
Sitting at his desk, he tried to block out his thoughts, but a sigh tinged with the remnants of tears escaped.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Is everything settled now?
He had left the Paranormal Psychic Science Research Society. Nade had been very angry but eventually hugged him, telling him to come back no matter what.
And today, he had said his goodbyes to Maya and Amy.
He wanted to sever all ties before going to Heaven.
No, he had to.
If he had any lingering attachments here, he might hesitate at a crucial moment. He might waver in a situation where he absolutely had to die.
In a short time, Shirone had managed to sever all his ties.
But the faces of his loved ones still floated in his mind.
Rian, Tess, and... his parents, who always wished for their son's happiness.
He was glad he had told them he loved them before leaving home.
That was truly the best thing Shirone had ever done in his life.
He opened the drawer and took out a clean sheet of paper. The farewells he hadn't been able to say would now have to be written.
Turning the pen in his fingers, Shirone finally began to write on the paper.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Will.
Sob. Sob.
Tears of sorrow streamed down his face.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Mom...
To Shirone, his parents were Vincent and Olina. That fact would never change.
He should have known before deciding to go to Heaven.
No, he shouldn't have met Ikael in the first place.
But he had found out.
He hadn't shown it to anyone, but the answer to the question he had carried in his heart all his life was with her.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm sorry, I'm so sorry.
Wiping his tears with his sleeve, Shirone continued writing his will.
He expressed gratitude to those who had helped him, shared that he was happy to have received so much love, and conveyed his respect for his parents.
In this way, he was emptying everything he had held inside for 19 years.
